title: Soaring ammunition cost turns Iran conflict into 'war of stockpiles'
subhead: The daily interception of thousands of missiles and drones launched by Tehran over the past three weeks has put severe strain on already limited reserves. The situation is particularly critical for France, which is bound by defense agreements with several Middle Eastern countries.

title: UNRWA head Philippe Lazzarini's grim assessment: 'Without firm political and financial support, we face a slow death'
US and Israel attack Iran's Natanz nuclear facility, says Iran atomic energy organization
France's aircraft carrier located in real time by Le Monde through Strava data
subhead: As the Charles de Gaulle and its strike group approach the Middle East, Le Monde identified a French sailor using the Strava fitness application in the Mediterranean Sea. This security flaw remains unaddressed despite our previous revelations.

title: 'With Operation Epic Fury, the US turns pop culture into the language of Pentagon violence'
subhead: In an op-ed, Louis Lapeyrie, a researcher at Paris-I Panthéon-Sorbonne University, examines the visual and narrative shifts in military communications under the Trump administration and explores the many borrowings from video games.

title: An ultra-modern artist: 'Renoir and Love' and 'Renoir Drawings' at the Musée d'Orsay
subhead: Challenging his reputation as a sentimental artist, two exhibitions of paintings and drawings highlight Renoir's affinity for working-class subjects and the complexity of his compositions.

title: As baby boomers pass on their wealth, young heirs grapple with guilt and discomfort
subhead: Receiving a large sum of money at a young age is anything but trivial. Early inheritances and gifts often bring discomfort, guilt and silence for those who benefit, and deepen inequalities.

subhead: 'Writers on the war in the Middle East' 1/4. Le Monde asked writers Richard Ford, Zeruya Shalev and Navid Kermani for their perspectives on the war.

title: In Paris, Reza Pahlavi, son of Iran's last shah, builds his network
subhead: Reza Pahlavi has been based in the French capital since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran started, making moves in anticipation of the Islamic Republic's fall.

title: Bertrand Badie, political scientist: 'Persian culture is an impetuous alliance of dignity and fury'
subhead: The international relations specialist, who is of Iranian descent on his father's side, speaks with Le Monde about the West's lack of understanding of Iran and its culture.

title: War in Iran: 'Europe said no to the US. This is new, and it is a major development'
